anchor.section: Heyting algebras as applied to intuitionistic logic
anchor.snippet: if a formula is deducible from the laws of intuitionistic logic

annotations.label: Peirce's law fails in three-element algebra

anchor.section: Heyting algebras as applied to intuitionistic logic
anchor.snippet: However one can construct a Heyting algebra in which the value of Peirce's law is not always 1

annotations.note: No counterexample to Peirce's law in a specific Heyting algebra is formalized.

annotations.label: Completeness of intuitionistic logic

anchor.section: Heyting algebras as applied to intuitionistic logic
anchor.snippet: if a formula always has the value 1, then it is deducible from the laws of intuitionistic logic

annotations.note: Algebraic completeness of intuitionistic propositional logic isn't in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Decidability of equational theory (Kripke)

anchor.snippet: The problem of whether a given equation holds in every Heyting algebra was shown to be decidable

annotations.note: Kripke's decidability result is not formalized.

annotations.label: PSPACE-completeness (Statman)

anchor.snippet: The precise computational complexity of the problem was established by Richard Statman

annotations.note: Statman's PSPACE-completeness is not formalized.

annotations.label: First-order theory is undecidable

anchor.snippet: The elementary or first-order theory of Heyting algebras is undecidable

annotations.note: Undecidability of the first-order theory of Heyting algebras isn't formalized.

annotations.label: Heyting algebras are not locally finite

anchor.snippet: it is known that Heyting algebras are not locally finite

annotations.note: Not formalized.

annotations.label: Topological representation of Heyting algebras

anchor.snippet: Every Heyting algebra H is naturally isomorphic to a bounded sublattice

annotations.note: No Stone-style representation theorem for Heyting algebras is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Duality with Heyting spaces

anchor.snippet: the category of Heyting algebras is dually equivalent to the category of Heyting spaces

annotations.note: Heyting duality isn't formalized.

annotations.label: Esakia duality

anchor.snippet: the category of Heyting algebras is dually equivalent to the category of Esakia spaces

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `EsakiaHom` (Esakia morphisms) but does not state or prove the duality.
